---
title: "36-671 Final Project"
author: "Ellie Huang, Nikita Jayaprakash, Annie Wang, Aishani Pradhan, Steve Wong"
date: "2026-04-19"
output: pdf_document
---

Research question: How do monthly gasoline prices differ across U.S. regions, and how well can national gasoline prices be modeled and forecasted using ARIMA methods?

```{r setup, include = F}
library(here)
library(readxl)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)

# This builds a path relative to the project root
file_path <- here("data", "gasoline_data.xls")
```

```{r}
# Read national series from Data 1
# skip = 2 because:
# row 1 = title row
# row 2 = source key row
# row 3 = actual variable names
us_data <- read_excel(file_path, sheet = "Data 1", skip = 2)

us_data <- us_data |>
  rename(
    Date = 1,
    US_All_Grades = 2
  ) |>
  mutate(Date = as.Date(Date))

# Read regional series from Data 2
region_data <- read_excel(file_path, sheet = "Data 2", skip = 2)

region_data <- region_data |>
  rename(
    Date = 1,
    East_Coast = 2,
    New_England = 3,
    Central_Atlantic = 4,
    Lower_Atlantic = 5,
    Midwest = 6,
    Gulf_Coast = 7,
    Rocky_Mountain = 8,
    West_Coast = 9,
    West_Coast_ex_CA = 10
  ) |>
  mutate(Date = as.Date(Date))

# Keep only the series we want for the project
gas_data <- us_data |>
  select(Date, US_All_Grades) |>
  left_join(
    region_data |>
      select(Date, East_Coast, Midwest, Gulf_Coast, Rocky_Mountain, West_Coast),
    by = "Date"
  )

# Restrict to the common date range where all selected series are observed
gas_complete <- gas_data |>
  drop_na()

# Check the resulting date range
min_date <- min(gas_complete$Date)
max_date <- max(gas_complete$Date)

cat("Common date range:", format(min_date, "%Y-%m"), "to", format(max_date, "%Y-%m"), "\n")
cat("Number of monthly observations:", nrow(gas_complete), "\n")

# Preview beginning and end of the restricted data
head(gas_complete)
tail(gas_complete)
```

```{r}
# Create the main time series for ARIMA modeling
main_df <- gas_complete |>
  select(Date, US_All_Grades)

start_year <- as.numeric(format(min(main_df$Date), "%Y"))
start_month <- as.numeric(format(min(main_df$Date), "%m"))

us_ts <- ts(
  main_df$US_All_Grades,
  start = c(start_year, start_month),
  frequency = 12
)

plot(
  us_ts,
  main = "U.S. All Grades Gasoline Prices",
  xlab = "Year",
  ylab = "Dollars per Gallon"
)

# Create regional ts objects using the same restricted sample
east_ts <- ts(gas_complete$East_Coast, start = c(start_year, start_month), frequency = 12)
midwest_ts <- ts(gas_complete$Midwest, start = c(start_year, start_month), frequency = 12)
gulf_ts <- ts(gas_complete$Gulf_Coast, start = c(start_year, start_month), frequency = 12)
rocky_ts <- ts(gas_complete$Rocky_Mountain, start = c(start_year, start_month), frequency = 12)
west_ts <- ts(gas_complete$West_Coast, start = c(start_year, start_month), frequency = 12)

ts.plot(
  east_ts, midwest_ts, gulf_ts, rocky_ts, west_ts,
  col = 1:5,
  lty = 1,
  xlab = "Year",
  ylab = "Dollars per Gallon",
  main = "Regional Monthly Gasoline Prices"
)

legend(
  "topleft",
  legend = c("East Coast", "Midwest", "Gulf Coast", "Rocky Mountain", "West Coast"),
  col = 1:5,
  lty = 1,
  bty = "n"
)
```
